rationale: Keep: the cited paper directly studies whether a consumer’s own Facebook brand action (clicking Like/joining) causally affects brand attitudes and purchasing, finding no positive causal lift and possible slight negatives. That closely overlaps with the citing paper’s core question about how product posting affects the poster’s own purchase interest, so this is plausibly a substantive theory/comparison anchor rather than an incidental mention (DOI-matched reference 10.1509/jmr.14.0237).
